titleStmt.title: Letter from James P. Farrell et al. to Augustine Birrell, 7 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James P. Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from James P. Farrell M.P for North Longford, Thomas Francis Smyth M.P. for South Leitrim, Francis Meehan M.P. for North Leitrim, Patrick Crumley M.P. for South Fermanagh and John Phillips M.P. for South Longford written on 7 April 1916. They are addressing Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), chief secretary for Ireland. The five MPs are appealing for Birrell to appoint a Mr Thomas W. Delaney to the office of chief Crown solicitor of Ireland. The position had become vacant following the sudden death of Sir Malachy Kelly on 25 March 1916. They claim that Delaney 'is best entitled to the office'.Augustine Birrell served as the Chief Secretary of Ireland from 1907 to 1916. Birrell was a liberal politician praised for his reforms of the Irish educational system. The Easter Rising caught him unawares, occupied in London, there was little he could do. A later inquiry into the Rising held Birrell chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Birrell offered his resignation on May 1.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6258/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
April 7. 1916 To the Right Hon A Birrell M.P. Chief Secretary for Ireland. Dear Mr Birrell We the undersigned members of
Parliament respectfully appeal to you
to appoint Mr Thomas W. Delaney
Crown Solicitor of Longford to the vacant
office of Chief Crown Solicitor of Ireland vacant
by the death of Sir Malachy Kelly.
We support Mr Delanys application
solely on the question of merit. He is
well known in the midlands of Ireland
to be a solicitor of Commanding ability
and force of character. He is well
known as a public man in Co.and
district councils and has rendered inestimable
service to the cause of recruiting in Ireland.
We therefore urge by claims on the
ground of personal merit and public service
& on these grounds we believe he as
a Crown solicitor with practical knowledge
of the work is best entitled to the office Signed James P. Farrell M.P. North Longford
Thos. F. Smyth M.P. South Leitrim
Francis E. Meehan M.P. N Leitrim
P Crumley M.P. S Fermanagh
John Phillips M.P. S Longford
